rulings.Case Title: RESURGENCE FINANCIAL, LLC VS. MELVIN A. POSADA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Execption
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2020, Line 1, ASSIGNEE COLLECT ACCESS LLC's Hearing On Claim Of Exemption. Defendant Posada's claim of exemption is granted. The levying officer is directed to release $1,000 of the funds held to the judgment creditor for payment on the judgment. All remaining funds shall be released to the debtor. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2020, Line 2, PETITIONER JOHN/JANE DOE's Motion To Quash Deposition Subpoena Duces Tecum And For Monetary Sanctions. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. rulings.Case Title: HILARY WARE ET AL VS. EAGLE PROPERTY SOLUTIONS, LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants, Intero Real Estate Services, Inc., Wachiraporn Wachiradejkul Aka Jane Wachiradejkul And Douglas H. Tobins Notice Of Joinder To Defendants Eric Tolman And Asdm Services, Inc. Dba Asdm Constructions Motion To Compel Site Inspection
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, August 21, 2020, Line 1, DEFENDANTS INTERO REAL ESTATE SERVICES, INC, WACHIRAPORN "JANE" WACHIRADEJKUL, AND DOUGLAS TOBIN'S Joinder to Defendants Eric Tolman and ASDM Services, Inc. dba ASDM Construction's Motion To Compel Site Inspection. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Joinder is granted. PARTIES TO APPEAR. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CORNERSTONE LAW GROUP VS. SUSAN BIBA
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, August 21, 2020, Line 5, DEFENDANT SUSAN BIBA's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT /Summary Adjudication. Defendant Susan Biba's summary judgment motion - now limited to the remaining fourth cause of action for quantum meruit - is denied. A defendant moving for summary adjudication must show that at least one element of a cause of action "cannot be established" or that "there is a complete defense" to the cause. (CCP section 437c(p)(2).) Defendant's memorandum does not mention the elements of quantum meruit (see California Civil Jury Instruction 371), so she apparently is not moving on that ground. Defendants' memo argues "breach of duty of loyalty" and "breach of fiduciary duty," but her answer pled neither as a defense. In any event, there are triable issues as to when plaintiff represented defendant (Lewis Dec. 5-16), and quantum meruit has long been recognized as an alternate means of recovery even when an attorney-client fee agreement is void. (Rosenberg v. Lawrence (1938) 10 Cal.2d 590, 593-94; Iverson, Yoakum, Papiano & Hatch v. Berwald (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 990, 996.) Whether recovery is appropriate in this case remains to be litigated. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/RBU)

rulings.Case Title: ADAM BRATT VS. EBERE ANOSIKE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Compel Futher Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Friday, August 21, 2020, Line 3, PLAINTIFF ADAM BRATT'S Motion To Compel Futher Response To Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One And For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Katherine Gallo, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is Granted. All objections are overruled. Defendant to provide a response in compliance with CCP 2031.210-230 and produce documents no later than September 8, 2020. If the documents responsive to this request has already been produced then defendants shall identify the documents by providing the description of the responsive documents, including the bates stamp number and the production date in order to be in compliance with CCP 2031.280(a). Plaintiff's request for sanctions is granted in the amount of $1975.00. Payment to be made to the Client Trust Fund of HENSHAW & HENRY, PC no later than September 8, 2020. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to Klgallo@discoveryreferee.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)
